section: BILINGUAL (ARABIC AND PUNJABI IN PERSIAN CHARACTER)—RELIGION.
author: Muhammad Najm-ud-din
title: Umda-tal Khitab fi Fazail-il-Ashab
gloss: A booklet describing the virtues and excellencies of Muhammad's companions, with quotations from the Quran and traditions, translated in Punjabi verse.

title: Majmua-i-Khutab-i-Kalan-i-Afghani
gloss: A collection of sermons recited immediately before the Juma and after the 'Id prayers.

title: Quran Sharif Mutarjam
gloss: The Quran with translation in Pushto.
copyright: H. Amir Muhammad, Pesháwar city; No. 15(?), dated the (?) September, 1910.
notes: Dual date: 1327 Hijri / May 1910. Complete Pushto Quran, 960 pp. quarto, printed in Lahore for the Peshawar market.

flags.issue: register number and day partially legible

author: Ahmad Shah, Mir, Razwani, S. U.
title: Arabi ki Tisri Kitab
gloss: Third Arabic Reader for the Middle Schools prepared by the author for the Text-Book Revision Committee, Punjab.
copyright: The Punjab Text-Book Committee, Lahore; No. 146, dated the 6th September, 1910.
notes: Section headed 'Designed for Educational Purposes.)'. Text-Book Committee Arabic reader series (regs 1100–1105, copyright Nos. 141–146).

author: Ahmad Shah, Mir, Razwani, S. U.
title: Arabi ki Chauthi Kitab
gloss: Fourth Arabic Reader for the High Department.
copyright: The Punjab Text-Book Committee, Lahore; No. 144, dated the 6th September, 1910.

title: Qawaid-i-Arabi
gloss: Arabic Grammar for the second class of Middle Schools prepared by the Text-Book Revision Committee, Punjab.
copyright: The Punjab Text-Book Committee, Lahore; No. 141, dated the 6th September, 1910.

author: Abdul Hamid, Hafiz
title: Faisalat-ul-Quran fi Taqlid-il-Quran
gloss: A pamphlet in which the writer tries to prove from the Quran that taqlid (the act of following a particular Imam) is allowed by the Quran and that the followers of the sect called the Hanafis profess the true religion.
pubcity: village Wan Abdul Hamid, Montgomery district
notes: Dual date: 1328 Hijri / 20th June 1910. Pro-taqlid (Hanafi) polemic — the Ahl-i-Hadis controversy from the other side. Serials in this section continue from Q2 (19 ff.). Price printed '1 aunas'.
